# DJZS — deterministic pre-execution audit for agents

> DJZS audits the reasoning behind an autonomous agent's move before capital is committed and
> returns a tri-state verdict — PASS, WAIT, or FAIL — with the reasoning defects flagged and a
> reproducible verdict_hash. It answers "should this position be taken?", complementary to
> transaction-security tools that answer "is this transaction safe to sign?".

## Use it (live MCP gate)

- Endpoint (streamable HTTP): https://mcp.djzs.ai/mcp
- Install: `claude mcp add --transport http djzs-trust https://mcp.djzs.ai/mcp`
- Lead tool: `verify_pm_trade` — pass a free-text prediction-market thesis; returns
  PASS→PROCEED / FAIL / WAIT→HALT, flags, unknown_fields, disagreements, and verdict_hash.
  Optional `agent_address` (0x wallet) links the verdict to that agent's on-chain trust record.
  Prediction-market only: a non-PM or undetermined intent returns in_scope:false (never a silent
  perpetuals audit).
- Other tools (free): `query_pol_certificates` (reads ProofOfLogic certificates from Irys mainnet,
  including every certificate verify_pm_trade anchors);
  `query_agent_trust` (LIVE — on-chain trust score read from the DJZS subgraph: totalAudits,
  pass/fail counts, failRate, latest verdict/risk, DJZS-S01/X01 flag counts, and an action of
  PROCEED, HALT, or NO_HISTORY; HALT if failRate > 0.3 or S01/X01 fired more than once).
- Paying: verify_pm_trade costs 2.00 USDC per audit on Base mainnet over x402. MCP-native payer:
  wrap your MCP client with withX402Client (agents/x402) using an account holding USDC on Base,
  network "base", and maxPaymentValue 2000000n — the library's default cap is 0.10 USDC and
  refuses this price. Gas is facilitator-paid. Full recipe: https://djzs.ai/guide.html#pay

## How it works

- Architecture C: an LLM extracts observable facts (N=3 consensus, quote gates, evidence-unanimity)
  and never returns a verdict; a frozen deterministic engine computes the verdict. The verdict rules
  are model-independent. Same input struct -> same verdict_hash.
- WAIT is never silent: an unknown decision-critical fact drains to HALT with unknown_fields returned.

## Taxonomies (frozen)

- DJZS-M (prediction market, DJZS-PM-v1.0): M01-M04, weights sum 100, FAIL threshold 25. All live.
- DJZS-LF (perpetuals, DJZS-LF-v1.1): 11 codes, weights sum 200, 3 wired live (X01, E01, I01).

## Live posture (stated honestly)

- verify_pm_trade is PAID: 2.00 USDC per audit over x402 (CDP facilitator) on Base mainnet,
  non-custodial (payer -> treasury via EIP-3009; the facilitator submits the transfer and pays gas).
  The registry tools (query_pol_certificates, query_agent_trust) are free.
- Certificates anchor to Irys MAINNET and are permanent; retrievable by id and via
  query_pol_certificates.
- The trust loop is CLOSED and live: pass agent_address to verify_pm_trade and the verdict is
  written on-chain (DJZSLogicTrustScore on Base) by a dedicated authorized writer, indexed by the
  DJZS subgraph on The Graph, and readable by anyone via query_agent_trust. The write is fail-open:
  a write fault never blocks or alters the audit or its certificate.
- Endpoint history: the gate previously served at djzs-trust-mcp.easy-less-spoil.workers.dev/mcp.
  That URL still works for existing clients; https://mcp.djzs.ai/mcp is canonical.

## Source of truth

- Repository: https://github.com/SIFR0-dev/djzs-AI
- Every number here traces to that tree; where docs and tree disagree, the tree wins.
